text:
Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. A key principle is responsive design, which ensures the app looks and works well on different screen sizes, from small phones to large tablets.

Developers must choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built specifically for one system, like iOS or Android, offering excellent performance and full access to device features. Cross-platform apps use a single codebase to work on both systems, which saves time and money but may not perform as smoothly.

Once completed, apps are distributed through official app stores like Google Play or Apple’s App Store. These platforms have strict guidelines to ensure quality and security. Ultimately, good performance—fast loading times, smooth animations, and minimal crashes—is crucial for user satisfaction.
